# neo

> neo is a CLI tool that deploys and manages Docker-based applications on remote servers over SSH.
> It handles the full lifecycle: server setup, deployment, SSL certificates, env vars, databases, and monitoring.
> All operations run locally and execute on the remote server via SSH — no agent required on the server.

## Key concepts

- **Server**: a remote host (e.g. `root@1.2.3.4`) registered in the local config at `~/.neo/config.json`
- **App**: a Dockerfile-based project deployed as a Docker container with a domain and auto-SSL
- **Service**: a shared database or cache (postgres, mysql, redis, mariadb) used by one or more apps
- **State**: persisted at `/etc/neo/state.json` on each server — source of truth for what is deployed

## Prerequisites

- neo installed locally: `curl -fsSL https://get.vxero.dev/neo/install.sh | sh`
- Remote server running Ubuntu 24.04+ or Debian (any version)
- SSH access to the server (key-based or password)
- A Dockerfile in the project directory (for `neo deploy`)

## Commands

### Server setup

```
neo init <user@host>          Initialize server: installs Docker, Caddy, sets up neo network
neo servers                   List all configured servers
neo use <name>                Switch active server
neo ssh                       Open interactive SSH session to current server
```

### Deploy an app

```
neo deploy [path]             Build and deploy a Dockerfile project (zero-downtime blue-green)
  --name <name>               App name (default: directory name)
  --domain <domain>           Domain for the app (e.g. app.example.com)
  --port <port>               Container port (default: auto-detected from Dockerfile EXPOSE)
  --env KEY=VALUE             Set env var at deploy time (repeatable)
  --env-file <file>           Load env vars from a .env file at deploy time
  --server <name>             Target a specific server
```

The deploy command:
1. Builds the Docker image on the remote server (streams build output)
2. Starts the new container
3. Registers the domain in Caddy (auto-provisions SSL via Let's Encrypt)
4. Saves app state to `/etc/neo/state.json`

### App lifecycle

```
neo list [--json]             List all apps and services (JSON: server, apps, services)
neo start <app>               Start a stopped app
neo stop <app>                Stop a running app
neo restart <app>             Restart an app
neo update <app>              Pull latest image and redeploy
neo remove <app>              Remove app container (data volumes preserved)
neo run <app> -- <cmd>        Run a one-off command inside the container
```

### Logs and monitoring

```
neo logs <app>                Stream app logs
  --tail <n>                  Show last N lines (default: 50)
  -f                          Follow (stream continuously)
neo status [--json]           Server health: CPU, RAM, disk, uptime, container stats
neo status --live             Live-updating metrics (refreshes every 3s)
```

### Domains and SSL

```
neo domain <app> <domain>     Set domain (triggers auto-SSL via Caddy + Let's Encrypt)
neo domain <app> --temp       Assign a free sslip.io domain with instant SSL (no DNS needed)
```

### Environment variables

```
neo env <app> [--json]        View env vars (secrets masked; JSON: {"app":"...","vars":{...}})
neo env set <app> KEY=VALUE   Set one or more env vars (auto-restarts container)
neo env unset <app> KEY       Remove an env var (auto-restarts container)
neo env import <app> <file>   Bulk import from a .env file (auto-restarts container)
```

Sensitive keys (containing SECRET, KEY, PASSWORD, TOKEN, PASS, PRIVATE, CREDENTIAL) are masked in output.

### Shared services (databases / caches)

```
neo service create <type> <name>      Create a shared service
  Types: postgres, mysql, redis, mariadb
  Example: neo service create postgres main-db

neo service list                      List services and their linked apps
neo service link <svc> <app>          Create a DB + user, inject DATABASE_URL and DB_* env vars into app
neo service unlink <svc> <app>        Remove link (data is preserved)
neo service start|stop|restart <svc>  Lifecycle management
neo service remove <svc>              Remove (must unlink all apps first)
neo service logs <svc>                Stream service logs
```

When a service is linked to an app, neo injects these env vars automatically:
- `DATABASE_URL` — full connection string
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_NAME`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`

### Data

```
neo backup <app>              Backup data volumes to a local archive
neo restore <app> <file>      Restore volumes from a backup archive
neo volumes                   List Docker volumes on the server
```

### Updates

```
neo version                   Show current version, check for updates
neo upgrade                   Download and replace neo binary with latest version
```

## Global flags

```
--server <name>               Target a specific configured server (overrides current)
```

## Machine-readable output (for LLM pipelines)

```
neo list --json               Apps and services inventory
neo status --json             Server health metrics and container stats
neo env <app> --json          Env vars for an app (secrets masked)
neo help --llm                Full plain-text command reference (no ANSI colors)
```

### neo list --json shape
```json
{
  "server": "production",
  "apps": {
    "ghost": { "name": "ghost", "image": "...", "domain": "blog.example.com", "status": "running", "internal_port": 2368 }
  },
  "services": {
    "main-db": { "name": "main-db", "image": "postgres:16", "status": "running", "linked_apps": { "ghost": {} } }
  }
}
```

### neo status --json shape
```json
{
  "server": "production",
  "host": "root@1.2.3.4",
  "reachable": true,
  "latency_ms": 12,
  "cpu_percent": "4.2",
  "ram_used_mb": "512",
  "ram_total_mb": "1024",
  "disk_used": "4.2G",
  "disk_total": "20G",
  "uptime": "3 days, 4 hours",
  "apps": { "running": 2, "stopped": 0, "total": 2 },
  "services": { "running": 1, "total": 1 },
  "containers": [
    { "name": "app-ghost", "cpu": "0.5%", "memory": "128MiB / 1GiB" }
  ]
}
```

### neo env \<app\> --json shape
```json
{
  "app": "ghost",
  "vars": {
    "MAIL_HOST": "smtp.example.com",
    "MAIL_PORT": "587",
    "SECRET_KEY": "ab**************************"
  }
}
```

## Common workflows

### Deploy a new app from scratch
```bash
neo init root@1.2.3.4          # one-time: set up the server
cd my-project                   # project must have a Dockerfile
neo deploy --domain app.example.com
```

### Deploy with a database
```bash
neo service create postgres main-db
neo deploy --domain app.example.com --name myapp
neo service link main-db myapp   # injects DATABASE_URL into myapp and restarts it
```

### Update environment variables
```bash
neo env set myapp MAIL_HOST=smtp.example.com MAIL_PORT=587
neo env import myapp .env.production
```

### Redeploy after code changes
```bash
neo deploy   # re-runs from current directory, zero-downtime
```

### Check what is running
```bash
neo list --json | jq '.apps'
neo status --json | jq '{cpu: .cpu_percent, ram_used: .ram_used_mb, ram_total: .ram_total_mb}'
```

### Troubleshoot a failing app
```bash
neo logs myapp --tail 100
neo status --json
neo env myapp --json
neo restart myapp
```

## .neo.yml (optional project config)

Place in the project root to set defaults for `neo deploy`:

```yaml
name: my-app              # app name (default: directory name)
domain: app.example.com   # domain
port: 8080                # container port
env_file: .env.production # load env vars from file
env:                      # non-sensitive env var defaults
  APP_ENV: production
  LOG_LEVEL: info
```

## Error patterns

- `app "name" not found` — the app is not deployed; run `neo list` to see deployed apps
- `no server configured` — run `neo init <user@host>` first
- `SSH connection refused` — check the server IP and that SSH is running; try `neo ssh`
- `Docker not found` — server needs initialization; run `neo init <user@host>`
- `OS not supported` — neo requires Ubuntu 24.04+ or Debian; other distros are not supported
